Research Results · Quantum Nonlinear Optics

Optimization of Entangled
Two-Photon Absorption

A comparative study of two-photon state probability distributions in the temporal and frequency domains for three photon-pair architectures: the optimum state, time-energy entangled pairs, and unentangled (classical) pairs. Each section presents interactive visualizations controlled by the physical parameters of the system.

Domain
Nonlinear quantum optics
States compared
Optimum · Entangled · Unentangled
Parameters
Γe/Γf · δa/Γf
Status
Fully self-contained — no server needed
scroll to explore
01 · Main result 6 panels

Two-photon State Probability Distributions

For each photon-pair architecture, we compute the joint two-photon probability distribution \(p(t_1, t_2)\) and \(p(\omega_1, \omega_2)\) together with their marginals \(P(t)\) and \(P(\omega)\). The slider for \(\Gamma_e/\Gamma_f\) and \(\delta_a/\Gamma_f\) controls which parameter row is displayed. The checkbox toggles between optimised and fixed \(\delta_f\).

State I (Optimal) Eq. (19) · p. 4
\[ \Phi_{\rm opt}(t_1,t_2)= \frac{e^{\frac{1}{2}\Gamma_{f} t_2 -\frac{1}{2}\Gamma_{e}(t_2-t_{1}) -i\omega_{fe}t_2-i\omega_{eg}t_1}} {\sqrt{\mathcal{N}_{\rm opt}}} \,\Theta(t-t_2)\,\Theta(t_2-t_1)\,\Theta(t_1-t_0) \]
State II Eq. (39) · p. 9
\[ \Phi(t_1,t_2)=\sqrt{\frac{\Omega_{+}\Omega_{-}}{2\pi}} \exp\!\left\{ -\frac{\Omega_{+}^2}{8}(t_1+t_2-\mu)^2 -\frac{\Omega_{-}^2}{8}\bigl(t_1-(t_2-\mu)\bigr)^2 -i\omega_{01}t_1-i\omega_{02}t_2 \right\} \]
State III Eq. (29) · p. 5
\[ \Phi_{\rm sym}(t_1,t_2)= \frac{1}{2\sqrt{\mathcal{N}}} \bigl[\psi_{a}(t_1)\psi_{b}(t_2) +\psi_{b}(t_1)\psi_{a}(t_2)\bigr] \]
Loading datasets…
Fig. 1 — Joint probability distributions and marginals for defined states (temporal & frequency domains)
—
\(\Gamma_e / \Gamma_f\) 0.0000
\(\delta_a / \Gamma_f\) 0.00
Optimum [−5, 0], others [−5, 5]
Each column corresponds to one photon-pair architecture. Rows: temporal marginal P(t), temporal joint distribution p(t₁, t₂), frequency marginal P(ω), and frequency joint distribution p(ω₁, ω₂). White dashed lines in the frequency heatmaps mark the intermediate-state resonances ωeg and ωfe.
02 · Spectral structure 3 panels

Peak Positions of the Marginal Frequency Distributions

The peak positions \(\omega_\text{peak}\) of the single-photon marginal distribution \(P(\omega)\) are plotted as functions of \(\Gamma_e/\Gamma_f\) for each state. Two peaks may emerge as the intermediate-state decay rate changes. Reference lines show the bare resonances \(\omega_{fe}\) and \(\omega_{eg}\).

State I (Optimal)— marginal distribution Eq. (27) · p. 5
\[ p(\omega)=\frac{\Gamma_{e}(\Gamma_{e}+\Gamma_{f})\!\left(\Gamma_{e}+\tfrac{1}{4}\Gamma_{f}\right) +\Gamma_{e}(\omega_{fe}-\omega_{eg})^2 +\Gamma_{f}(\omega-\omega_{eg})^2} {4\pi \left[(\omega-\omega_{eg})^2+\tfrac{\Gamma_{e}^2}{4}\right] \left[(\omega-\omega_{fe})^2+\tfrac{(\Gamma_{e}+\Gamma_{f})^2}{4}\right]} \]
State II — marginal distribution Eq. (44) · p. 9
\[ p(\omega)= \frac{1}{\sqrt{\pi(\Omega_{+}^2+\Omega_{-}^2)}\,\mathcal{N}} \Biggl\{ e^{-\frac{4(\omega-\omega_{01})^2}{\Omega_{+}^2+\Omega_{-}^2}} +e^{-\frac{4(\omega-\omega_{02})^2}{\Omega_{+}^2+\Omega_{-}^2}} +2e^{-\frac{4(\omega-\bar{\omega})^2}{\Omega_{+}^2+\Omega_{-}^2} -\Omega^2\mu^2 -\frac{\delta_f^2}{\Omega_{-}^2}} \cos\!\left[\frac{2\Omega_{-}^2\mu}{\Omega_{+}^2+\Omega_{-}^2}(\omega-\bar{\omega})\right] \Biggr\} \]
State III — marginal distribution Eq. (37) · p. 6
\[ p(\omega)=\frac{1}{2\mathcal{N}} \left\{ \left|\tilde{\psi}_{a}(\omega)\right|^2 +\left|\tilde{\psi}_{b}(\omega)\right|^2 +2\,\mathrm{Re}\!\left(\tilde{\psi}_{a}^{*}(\omega)\,\tilde{\psi}_{b}(\omega)\,x\right) \right\} \] \[ x=\sqrt{\tfrac{2\Omega_a\Omega_b}{\Omega_a^2+\Omega_b^2}} \exp\!\left\{ -\tfrac{\Omega_a^2\Omega_b^2\mu^2+4\delta_f^2 -4i\delta_f(\mu_a\Omega_a^2+\mu_b\Omega_b^2)} {4(\Omega_a^2+\Omega_b^2)} \right\} \]

03 · Optimum state detail 1 panel

Peak Position of the Optimum-State Marginal Distribution

A focused view of the optimum-state frequency marginal peak positions derived analytically from Eq. (33) of the draft. Root-finding of the derivative locates the extrema as a function of \(\Gamma_e/\Gamma_f\) for the selected detuning \(\delta_a/\Gamma_f\).

Marginal distribution of State I (optimal) Eq. (27) · p. 5
\[ p(\omega)=\frac{\Gamma_{e}(\Gamma_{e}+\Gamma_{f})\!\left(\Gamma_{e}+\tfrac{1}{4}\Gamma_{f}\right) +\Gamma_{e}(\omega_{fe}-\omega_{eg})^2 +\Gamma_{f}(\omega-\omega_{eg})^2} {4\pi \left[(\omega-\omega_{eg})^2+\tfrac{\Gamma_{e}^2}{4}\right] \left[(\omega-\omega_{fe})^2+\tfrac{(\Gamma_{e}+\Gamma_{f})^2}{4}\right]} \]

Peak 1 (gold) and Peak 2 (sky blue) are roots of dP(ω)/dω = 0 computed numerically. The red dashed line marks ωfe/Γf = δa/(2Γf); the blue dashed line marks ωeg/Γf = −δa/(2Γf). Data from peak_data.json.
04 · Methods & notes

Computational notes

All visualizations run client-side; no server is required beyond a local file host for the JSON datasets. The JavaScript implementations of the probability functions mirror the analytical expressions derived in the accompanying paper.

Section 1 — Two-photon probability distributions

Calculated on a 200×200 grid using explicit meshgrid evaluation. Complex arithmetic is handled via math.js. The Plotly layout uses a 4-row × 3-column subplot structure. Heatmaps use a reversed Hot colorscale. Grid size and domain can be adjusted via the domain control panel.

  • Optimum state: Analytical expressions in temporal and frequency domains with the two-photon resonance at \(\omega_{fg}\).
  • Entangled state: Gaussian–Gaussian joint spectral amplitude parameterised by pump bandwidth \(\Omega_P\) and correlation bandwidth \(\Omega_M\), with optional delay \(t_{02}\).
  • Unentangled state: Symmetrised product state of two independent Gaussian wave-packets \(\psi_A\) and \(\psi_B\).

Parameters per slider combination are embedded directly in this file.

Section 2 — Peak positions, three states

Roots of the derivative of the marginal distribution \(P(\omega)\) are computed numerically. Each dataset (peak_data_Opt.json, peak_data_Ent.json, peak_data_UnEnt.json) contains pre-calculated peak positions over a grid of \((\Gamma_e/\Gamma_f,\, \delta_a/\Gamma_f)\) values. The slider selects the nearest available \(\delta_a\) row with tolerance 0.5.

Section 3 — Optimum peak positions (full detuning range)

Same root-finding methodology applied to the closed-form expression of the optimum-state marginal (Eq. 33 of the draft). The slider covers \(\delta_a/\Gamma_f \in [0, 100]\) with 0.25 step resolution. Data source: peak_data.json.

Libraries & dependencies

  • Plotly 2.27 — Section 1 heatmap subplot
  • Chart.js 4.4 — Sections 2 & 3 line charts
  • math.js 11.11 — Complex-number arithmetic
  • MathJax 3 — Inline LaTeX rendering

Keywords & references

This page will be updated as results are finalised. The theoretical framework and computational models are based on the accompanying manuscript (DOI to be linked).

entangled two-photon absorption ETPA cross-section SPDC joint spectral amplitude Schmidt decomposition time–energy entanglement marginal distribution optimum state variational optimization quantum nonlinear optics
